geography: Kangaroos live in Australia.

The Dead Sea is the lowest point on land.

The Danube River flows through ten countries.
Madagascar is the fourth largest island in the world.
The Mariana Trench is the deepest part of the ocean.
The Gobi Desert is located in Mongolia and China.
The Mississippi River is the second longest river in North America.
Greenland is the largest island in the world.
The Caspian Sea is the largest enclosed body of water.
The Alps stretch across eight European countries.
The Congo River is the deepest river in the world.
The Atacama Desert is one of the driest places on Earth.
The Great Lakes contain 21 percent of the world fresh surface water.
The Ural Mountains separate Europe and Asia.
The Strait of Gibraltar connects the Atlantic Ocean and Mediterranean Sea.
The Yangtze is the longest river in Asia.
The Ring of Fire circles the Pacific Ocean.
Mount Kilimanjaro is the tallest mountain in Africa.
The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.
The Bermuda Triangle is in the western Atlantic Ocean.
The Volga is the longest river in Europe.
Hawaii is the most isolated island chain on Earth.
The Suez Canal connects the Mediterranean and Red Seas.
The Himalayas contain the tallest peaks in the world.
Fiji is an island nation with over 300 islands.
The Nile is traditionally considered the longest river in the world.
The Pyrenees Mountains separate France and Spain.
Lake Victoria is the largest lake in Africa.
The Maldives is the flattest country in the world.
The Zambezi River feeds Victoria Falls.
The Galapagos Islands are located on the equator.

The Rhine River flows through western Europe.
New Zealand has more sheep than people.

The Appalachian Mountains are among the oldest in the world.
Iceland sits on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.
The Tigris and Euphrates rivers flow through Mesopotamia.
The Tundra biome covers about 20 percent of the Earth surface.

The Aral Sea has shrunk dramatically since the 1960s.
The Black Forest is located in southwestern Germany.
The Ross Ice Shelf is the largest ice shelf in Antarctica.
The Bay of Bengal is the largest bay in the world.
The Great Rift Valley stretches across eastern Africa.
science: The speed of light is approximately 300000 kilometers per second.
Absolute zero is minus 273.15 degrees Celsius.
A neutron has no electric charge.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
The periodic table has 118 confirmed elements.
Protons carry a positive electric charge.
The speed of sound in air is about 343 meters per second.
A photon has zero rest mass.
Newton published the Principia in 1687.
Electrons orbit the nucleus of an atom.
The Kelvin scale starts at absolute zero.
Plasma is the fourth state of matter.
Hubble Space Telescope was launched in 1990.
The half-life of carbon-14 is about 5730 years.
A parsec is about 3.26 light years.
The Doppler effect changes the frequency of waves.
Superconductors have zero electrical resistance.
Dark matter makes up about 27 percent of the universe.
The pH scale ranges from 0 to 14.
An isotope has the same number of protons but different neutrons.
The Higgs boson was confirmed in 2012.
A joule is the SI unit of energy.
Centripetal force acts toward the center of a circular path.
Alpha particles consist of two protons and two neutrons.
The Fibonacci sequence appears in many natural patterns.
An ampere is the SI unit of electric current.
Entropy measures the disorder of a system.
Gamma rays have the shortest wavelength in the electromagnetic spectrum.
A watt is one joule per second.
Avogadro number is approximately 6.022 times ten to the 23.
Ohm law relates voltage current and resistance.
A mole is a unit of measurement for amount of substance.
Quarks are fundamental particles that make up protons.
Thermodynamics has four laws numbered zero through three.
Planck constant is about 6.626 times ten to the negative 34.
The electromagnetic spectrum includes radio waves to gamma rays.
A tesla is the SI unit of magnetic flux density.
Bernoulli principle explains lift on airplane wings.
Quantum entanglement links particles across distance.
The standard model describes fundamental particles and forces.
Faraday law describes electromagnetic induction.
An electron volt is a unit of energy used in particle physics.
Brownian motion is the random movement of particles in fluid.
The cosmological constant was introduced by Einstein.
Refraction causes a straw to appear bent in water.
Pascal is the SI unit of pressure.
A black body absorbs all electromagnetic radiation.
Kirchhoff laws apply to electrical circuits.
Boyle law states that pressure and volume are inversely proportional.
The uncertainty principle was formulated by Heisenberg.
biology: Dolphins are mammals that live in the ocean.
Spiders have eight legs.
Mushrooms are a type of fungus.
Penguins cannot fly.
Octopuses have three hearts.
Sharks are fish with cartilaginous skeletons.
Bats are the only mammals capable of true flight.

Hummingbirds can fly backwards.
Starfish can regenerate lost arms.
Cheetahs are the fastest land animals.
Frogs undergo metamorphosis from tadpole to adult.
Venus flytraps are carnivorous plants.
Jellyfish have existed for over 500 million years.
Blue whales are the largest animals ever to have lived.
Coral is an animal not a plant.
Ants can carry objects 50 times their own body weight.
Tardigrades can survive extreme temperatures and radiation.
Owls can rotate their heads up to 270 degrees.
Seahorses are the only fish where males give birth.
Honey bees communicate through dance.
Sloths move so slowly that algae grows on their fur.
Chameleons change color for communication not camouflage.
Elephants are the largest living land animals.

Koalas sleep up to 22 hours a day.

Woodpeckers can peck up to 20 times per second.
